name: 09-stop-max-tokens
description: Canonical scenario #9: max_tokens terminal path. PARITY FINDING — Anthropic SDK auto-continues on stop_reason=max_tokens by injecting a synthetic 'Output token limit hit. Resume directly…' user message and firing a 2nd turn (observed in the request log); the OR /responses agent loop terminates after one response on status=completed with NO continuation. This is a real structural SDK divergence at the agent-loop layer, not a wire-level signal mismatch. v1 scripts both sides accordingly (3 Anthropic entries: title-gen + max_tokens turn + resume turn; 1 OR entry with status=completed) and asserts the parity that DOES hold: both terminate with success and emit a final assistant text without throwing. Hook-order strict equality is opted out via `skipHookOrderCheck` (load-bearing exception, scoped to this scenario by the flag's doc-comment in scenarios.ts); the comparator's `skipEventStreamCheck` is reused for the same reason. Ambiguity call #1 from issue 127: this is the SDK-side-NORMALIZED variant (both reach terminal:success) — tighter assertion would require either emulator `response.incomplete` support to test OR-side max_tokens mapping OR an Anthropic option to disable auto-continuation; both deferred to a follow-up.
systemPrompt: You are a helpful assistant. Keep responses concise.

content.text: TypeScript is a strongly-typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to plain
JavaScript. The previous response hit the token cap mid-sentence — stopping here.
